commit.message:
Embed TUI screenshots inline in PR comments for mobile viewing (#56)

* Add ratatui TUI frontend for goldfish pilot mode

Adds a terminal UI for playing goldfish games interactively using
ratatui and crossterm. Navigate zones (hand, battlefield, command zone,
stack, graveyard, exile) with W/S keys, browse cards within zones with
A/D, activate cards with Space, and undo with U. The TUI auto-advances
through goldfish turns and auto-pass situations.

Behind the `tui` feature flag: `cargo run --features tui --bin tui`

https://claude.ai/code/session_01J1WWfSKtxYRZnfvdWxWJiQ

* Add automated TUI screenshot testing for PR reviews

Extract TUI logic into library module (src/tui.rs) to enable headless
rendering via ratatui TestBackend. Add Buffer-to-SVG renderer that
converts styled terminal output into colored SVG images. Add snapshot
binary (tui_snapshot) that generates screenshots at key game states:
opening hand, zone navigation, card selection, mid-game, command zone.

Add GitHub Actions workflow that runs on TUI-related PRs, generates
screenshots for multiple decks, uploads as artifacts, and comments a
summary table on the PR for reviewer inspection.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01J1WWfSKtxYRZnfvdWxWJiQ

* Embed TUI screenshots inline in PR comments for mobile viewing

Convert SVGs to PNGs via rsvg-convert in CI (no new Rust deps), push
PNGs to a dedicated `tui-screenshots` orphan branch, then reference
them via raw.githubusercontent.com URLs in the PR comment. Each
scenario is wrapped in a collapsible <details> block so reviewers
can expand individual screenshots on mobile or desktop.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01J1WWfSKtxYRZnfvdWxWJiQ

* Fix screenshot workflow: avoid git checkout after orphan branch switch

The `git checkout -` failed because git loses the previous branch
reference after creating/switching to an orphan branch. Fix by:
- Computing the output URL before switching branches
- Saving PNGs to /tmp before the branch switch
- Dropping the checkout back (not needed, next step uses github-script)
- Pointing the comment script at /tmp/tui_pngs

https://claude.ai/code/session_01J1WWfSKtxYRZnfvdWxWJiQ

* Switch to ANSI-in-markdown for PR screenshot embeds

raw.githubusercontent.com returns 404 for private repos since camo
can't authenticate. Replace the SVG→PNG→branch→image-URL pipeline
with direct ANSI embedding in PR comments using GitHub's ```ansi
fenced code block rendering.

- Add buffer_to_ansi() that converts ratatui Buffer to ANSI SGR codes
- Add render_snapshot_ansi() and SnapshotOutput with both SVG + ANSI
- Snapshot binary now outputs .ansi files alongside .svg
- Workflow simplified: no rsvg-convert, no branch push, no image
  hosting — just reads .ansi files and embeds them in the PR comment
  inside collapsible <details> blocks

https://claude.ai/code/session_01J1WWfSKtxYRZnfvdWxWJiQ

* Revert "Switch to ANSI-in-markdown for PR screenshot embeds"

This reverts commit 92c77bfdc297449e76e56c69fd752dabce7bf70f.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sidebar navigation order + archive cascade (#258)

* Fix sidebar navigation order + archive cascade to offloaded children

Navigation (next/prev session shortcuts) now follows the sidebar's visual
section order instead of the main-process sort order, which diverged when
archived-with-alive-children or overflow recent sessions were present.

Auto-archive offloaded child sessions when their parent is archived.
Previously, sub-agent sessions that were offloaded before the parent died
would stay in "Recent" forever since the cascade only handled alive children.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify: remove redundant cascade from archiveSession, add early-exit

The archive cascade for offloaded children now lives only in
getOffloadedSessions() (session-discovery), which runs on every cache
miss. Removed the duplicate from archiveSession() in pool-manager.

Added hasOffloaded early-exit to skip graph reading when no offloaded
sessions exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add security hardening: CORS, rate limiting, body limits, and timeouts (#127)

* feat(serve): add server hardening (CORS, body limits, timeouts, graceful shutdown)

- CORS: configurable cross-origin policy with preflight handling, origin
  matching (string, array, function), credentials, and caching support.
  Enabled via `cors: true` or `cors: { origin, methods, ... }` in ServeConfig.

- Body limits: enforce max request body size (default 1MB) in the Node
  adapter. Returns 413 Payload Too Large when exceeded.

- Request timeouts: race handler execution against a configurable deadline
  (default 30s) in the Node adapter. Returns 504 Gateway Timeout on expiry.

- Graceful shutdown: track in-flight requests, stop accepting new
  connections on shutdown signal, drain with a configurable timeout
  (default 10s), then force-close remaining connections.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0132EL9eB8DPVp8yMmmmc9xE

* feat(serve): add rate limiting, ServeHttpError class, and error sanitization

- Rate limiting: pluggable middleware with in-memory store (token bucket
  per-window). Supports per-IP, per-tenant, or custom key functions.
  Configurable window, max requests, and fail-open behavior. Returns 429
  with Retry-After and X-RateLimit-* headers.

- ServeHttpError: typed error class that handlers/middleware can throw to
  return specific HTTP status codes and error types to the client, instead
  of relying on the catch-all 500 path.

- Error sanitization: the pipeline catch block no longer leaks
  error.message (which may contain stack traces, SQL, or internal paths)
  to the client. Raw errors remain available via the onError lifecycle
  hook for logging/observability.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0132EL9eB8DPVp8yMmmmc9xE

* docs(serve): document security hardening features

Add a Security hardening section to the serve API reference covering:
- CORS config (CorsConfig, preflight, credentials)
- Rate limiting (rateLimit(), MemoryRateLimitStore, custom Redis store)
- Body limits and request timeouts (bodyLimit, requestTimeout)
- Graceful shutdown (gracefulShutdownTimeout)
- ServeHttpError and error sanitization behaviour

Also add cors and security fields to the ServeConfig TypeTable, and
update the ErrorEnvelope type list with new error codes.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0132EL9eB8DPVp8yMmmmc9xE

* fix(serve): preserve error messages for in-process execution

Add sanitizeErrors option to executeEndpoint (default: true) so that:
- HTTP requests still get sanitized error messages (security)
- In-process execute() calls preserve original error messages for debugging

Also convert the tenantOptional misconfiguration error to ServeHttpError
so it surfaces properly in both HTTP and in-process contexts.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0132EL9eB8DPVp8yMmmmc9xE

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: wrap getSession in try/catch in login and signup pages

getSession() calls createSupabaseServerClient() which throws a ReferenceError
if Supabase env vars are missing, crashing the Server Component render with

falls through to render the auth form instead of crashing. redirect() is kept

commit.message:
Rewrite schedule/date relationship: schedule days are source of truth

- Schedule days stored as array of { date, discount } objects
- End date is read-only mirror of last schedule day's date
- TBD unchecked by default; end date shows today on load
- + Add always visible, opens native date picker to select date first
- New day inserts chronologically, day numbers recalculate
- Day dates are tappable to edit via date picker, re-sorts on change
- Swipe-to-delete on schedule rows (same pattern as item rows)
- Day 1 cannot be deleted (minimum 1 day always)
- End date tappable to add new last day; rejects dates before existing days
- Start date changes sync Day 1; Day 1 changes sync start date
- Duplicate date validation on all date operations

Fix overly broad glob pattern causing timestamp collisions

Removed "*{search_id}*.png" pattern that was matching files where design IDs appeared anywhere in filename, including timestamps. This was causing design_368_1764246343.png to match when searching for design "463" because "463" appeared in the timestamp.

Preserved legitimate patterns:
- design_{search_id}_*.png (standard multi-view)
- {search_id}.png (simple format)
- design_*_{search_id}.png (view-suffix format)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

fix: resolve AI refusing 'yes' by injecting synthetic greeting context

The widget greeting is rendered client-side only (never stored in DB),
so OpenAI received bare 'yes' with no conversation context and its own
strict rules caused it to fire the refusal message repeatedly.

Two fixes in lib/ai.ts:
1. Inject a synthetic first assistant turn (the greeting) when history
   is empty, giving GPT context that 'yes' is a response to the greeting
2. Add HANDLING SHORT REPLIES rule to system prompt so GPT treats all
   one-word affirmatives as booking intent, never as off-topic

Fix Jieqi rawValue overflow bugs and add regression tests

- Date.jieqi and Jieqi.current: add % 24 so values in [24, 24.5) from
  currentSolarTerm() wrap correctly to chunfen instead of returning nil.
  Affects ~March 12–20 each year (sun within 7.5° of Spring Equinox).
- Jieqi.jieqiPairs: add % 24 to the qi rawValue so the last pair
  correctly returns (jingzhe, chunfen) instead of (jingzhe, lichun).
- Jieqi.dizhi: replace ceil-based formula with ((rawValue / 2 + 2) % 12) + 1,
  fixing wrong Dizhi for all even rawValues and nil returns for
  xiaohan (19) and dahan (20).

Add JieqiTests covering all three bugs: near-equinox date, jieqiPairs
completeness, and full Jieqi→Dizhi mapping.

commit.message:
Fix suspend logic: full-suspend days + time window on active days

Semantics of `days` field is now: days the device is fully OFF.
- Day in `days` list (e.g. Sat/Sun) → suspended ALL DAY
- Day NOT in list (e.g. Mon-Fri) → suspended only during time window
- next_wake_time skips forward past full-suspend days to find
  the first active day where the wake time (end) applies

Example: active 09-18 Mon-Fri → device OFF from Fri 18:00 to Mon 09:00

commit.message:
Fix ambient agent: always show agent output, ambient adds commentary on top

Previously when ambient was enabled, agent streaming output was ONLY sent
to the ambient buffer and NOT displayed to the user. If ambient API calls
failed (rate limits), the user saw nothing at all.

Now agent output always streams to stderr (visible to user), and ambient
gets a copy to analyze. Ambient status updates show as [narrator] prefix
to distinguish from agent output.

commit.message:
fix(cli): complete plugin system — wire hooks, expand aliases, fix pipeline syntax (#229)

Resolves three categories of dead code identified in issue #157:

1. **Pipeline syntax corruption** (`pipeline.rs`): The `PipelineCommands::Run`
   match arm had two overlapping destructuring patterns from an incomplete merge
   of the idempotent-pipeline feature (#221). Fixed by merging both patterns into
   a single correct arm that binds all fields (file, ignore_failure, dry_run, var,
   max_parallel, resume, reset, reset_from) and calls run_pipeline with the right
   argument order. Also fixed the corrupted sequential-step loop body where an
   unclosed if-block caused duplicate execute_step calls and a stray state.mark
   call in the parallel branch.

2. **Plugin system completion** (`plugins.rs`, `main.rs`):
   - `run_pre_hooks` and `run_post_hooks` were dead (marked #[allow(dead_code)]).
     Now called in execute_command() around every command dispatch.
   - `get_alias` was dead. Now used in the interactive shell loop to expand
     configured aliases before parsing the command line with clap.
   - Removed all three #[allow(dead_code)] attributes — none are needed now.

3. **MCP auth guidance cleanup** (`auth_guidance.rs`, `tools_oss.rs`):
   - Removed unused items: SETUP_INSTRUCTIONS, THREE_LEGGED_PROMPT, get_auth_state,
     and the AuthState fields has_client_id / has_client_secret / three_legged_expired
     (never read in non-test code). Removed dead methods has_any_credentials and
     has_complete_2leg_credentials from AuthState.
   - Removed stale #[allow(dead_code)] from AuthRequirement, AuthState, and
     get_tool_auth_requirement — all are used in docs.rs and tools_oss.rs.
   - Simplified AuthState to {two_legged_valid, three_legged_valid} — the only
     fields actually consumed by get_tool_availability_summary.
   - Updated tools_oss.rs construction site and tests to match simplified struct.

4. **Duplicate Cargo dependency** (`raps-cli/Cargo.toml`):
   Removed duplicate `glob.workspace = true` entry (lines 119 and 134) that
   caused `error: duplicate key` on cargo load.

cargo check -p raps-cli now completes with zero errors and zero warnings.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move upload processing pipeline to Web Worker

JSON.parse + normalize + dedup + parseStreamingHistory now run in
uploadWorker.js off the main thread. For heavy users (300k+ entries),
this eliminates the ~3-4s main thread freeze during upload — the
spinner stays animated and the tab stays responsive throughout.

commit.message:
feat(cli): parallel bulk object downloads with concurrency control and multi-progress (#225)

Add `raps object download-bulk <prefix>` subcommand that:
- Lists all objects matching a given key prefix in an OSS bucket
- Downloads them in parallel using tokio::task::JoinSet with a
  configurable semaphore (--concurrency, default 4)
- Shows a multi-progress display: one per-file bar plus an overall
  progress bar via indicatif::MultiProgress
- Supports --flat to dump all files into the output directory without
  preserving the key prefix directory structure
- Skips files that already exist locally with a matching size
  (--skip-existing, enabled by default)
- Reports a final summary: N downloaded, M skipped, K failed, total
  bytes transferred, and elapsed time

Also fix two pre-existing merge-conflict residues in pipeline.rs that
prevented the crate from compiling (duplicate match arm and dangling
state-update code in the parallel step block), and remove a duplicate
`glob` entry in raps-cli/Cargo.toml.

Closes #163

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(kernel): HTTP/2 multiplexing for APS calls, optional HTTP/3 QUIC for swarm (#224)

- Add `http2` field to `HttpClientConfig` (default true); respect
  `RAPS_HTTP2=0` env var to opt out of H2 adaptive window.
- Add `create_swarm_client()` gated behind `#[cfg(feature = "h3")]`
  that builds a QUIC/HTTP3 reqwest client for swarm inter-agent transport;
  falls back gracefully to the standard H2 client when the feature is absent.
- Add `quic_enabled()` helper that reads `RAPS_SWARM_QUIC=1`.
- Expose `h3 = ["reqwest/http3"]` optional feature in raps-kernel and
  `h3 = ["raps-kernel/h3"]` in raps-cli.
- Add `--quic` / `RAPS_SWARM_QUIC` flag to `raps swarm worker start`;
  selects the QUIC client when the `h3` feature is compiled in, or prints
  a clear warning and falls back to HTTP/2 when it is not.
- Document transport configuration in module-level doc comments in http.rs.
- Add unit tests for H2 env opt-out, QUIC env enable, and swarm client fallback.

Closes #164

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
